title: centaurea leucophaea subsp. pseudocoerulescens
intro: Centaurea leucophaea subsp. pseudocoerulescens, also known as a subspecies of mountain centaury or mountain centaurea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the aster family, typically found in mountainous regions. The medicinal properties of Centaurea leucophaea subsp. pseudocoerulescens have been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. This plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and serving as an anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Centaurea leucophaea subsp. pseudocoerulescens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
